title: 9D6 Quest - a dice management, fantasy adventure game from the creator of Four Against Darkness
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9_6yRxY8iiY

Daniel (@dungeondive) explores 9D6 Quest, a light standalone dice management game by Andrea Sfiligoi that shares DNA with Four Against Darkness while offering a completely different experience. You build a single hero over nine sequential missions, each involving a randomized hex map and a specific objective—escort merchants, hunt magical items, uncover mysteries. The core mechanic revolves around managing your pool of nine six-sided dice, which degrade from fresh to tired to wounded to dead as you fail tests and endure combat.

With ten character classes (each offering unique class abilities), six hex maps, various major and minor foes, collectible magical items, and spells, there's surprising variety for a 70-page rulebook. Character choices like the Ranger offer power through difficulty reduction, making some paths easier than others. Special hexes like villages, temples, and dungeons provide healing opportunities and risk-reward decisions—do you venture into danger for treasure points to heal dice later, or play it safe? The game delivers portable, press-your-luck adventure that's perfect for travel or when you want something lighter than a full Four Against Darkness campaign.

----

Does the idea of managing a single hero character across nine missions appeal to you more than Four Against Darkness's party-focused approach?
